# Contract — `AnalyticsSession`

**Feature**: `003-session-tracking`
**Date**: 2026-05-18
**Stability**: public; new in slice 003. Pinned via the existing `PublicSurfacePinningTests` baseline (regenerated by slice 003).

The consumer-facing immutable projection of a session row. The shape `IAnalyticsEventStateProvider.CurrentSession` returns; the shape future-slice readers consume to attribute the current pageview to a session.

## Namespace

```
Analyzer.Analytics.AnalyticsSession
```

Lives in the same pinned namespace as `IAnalyticsEventStateProvider` and `AnalyticsEventReceipt`. Pinning captures it directly without transitive-reference walking (slice-002 U2 precedent).

## Shape

```csharp
namespace Analyzer.Analytics;

public sealed record AnalyticsSession(
    Guid SessionKey,
    Guid VisitorProfileKey,
    DateTimeOffset StartUtc,
    DateTimeOffset LastActivityUtc,
    DateTimeOffset? EndUtc,
    int PageviewCount,
    bool IsActive);
```

Seven properties, exposed positionally + via init-only accessors per the record-shape default. Full XML doc comments are required on the production type (see [`data-model.md`](../data-model.md) §5 for the comment text).

### Property semantics

| Property | Type | Semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `SessionKey` | `Guid` | Publicly-exposed stable identifier. Same value as `analyzerSession.sessionKey` and `analyzerEventReceipt.sessionKey` for receipts attributed to this session. The handle a consumer correlates across surfaces. |
| `VisitorProfileKey` | `Guid` | Hard FK to `customizerVisitorProfile.Key`. Always non-empty — the resolver short-circuits on empty visitor keys (inherited from slice-002 handler skip). |
| `StartUtc` | `DateTimeOffset` | When the session opened — the `receivedUtc` of the first pageview attached to it. Set once at insert; never changes. |
| `LastActivityUtc` | `DateTimeOffset` | When the most recent attached pageview was observed. Advances on every `Extend` operation. For a closed session, this is the `receivedUtc` of the last pageview, NOT the `endUtc`. |
| `EndUtc` | `DateTimeOffset?` | Logical close time — `LastActivityUtc + InactivityTimeoutMinutes`. NOT `now` (sweeper observation time); the logical-close-time semantic is load-bearing for session-duration analytics. Null while the session is still active. |
| `PageviewCount` | `int` | Number of pageviews attached to the session. **May exceed** `COUNT(*) FROM analyzerEventReceipt WHERE sessionKey = …` under slice-002 back-pressure: if a receipt write op is dropped due to queue capacity, the session's `pageviewCount` still increments because session resolution happens before the receipt enqueue. Spec edge case explicit. |
| `IsActive` | `bool` | True while the session is open; false once closed (lazy-close or sweeper). |

### What's intentionally absent

- **`deviceKey`** — server-side UA-hash artefact. Internal-only. See [`data-model.md`](../data-model.md) §5 "Why deviceKey is NOT on the public record" for rationale.
- **`anonymizedUtc`** — soft-anonymise marker. Internal to the cascade-step contract; consumers don't need it. (If a future slice surfaces "this session was anonymised" status, that's an additive property of this record. Slice 003 does not surface it.)
- **`id`** — the opaque DB PK. Never publicly exposed (slice-002 convention; `SessionKey` is the public stable handle).

### Why a record (not a class or struct)

Records give us:

1. Immutability by default (init-only accessors).
2. Value-based equality and `GetHashCode`, which is the right semantic for analytics projections.
3. `with`-expression cloning for the rare case a consumer wants a derived projection.
4. Pinning-baseline shape that's easy to read and diff against.

A struct was considered (parallels slice-001's `VisitorIdentity`) but rejected — `AnalyticsSession` carries 7 fields including reference types (no reference types here, but the rules differ for future additions); a record is the safer default for a public type that may grow. A class was rejected for mutability risk.

## Construction

`AnalyticsSession` is constructed in two places:

1. **`AnalyzerSessionResolver.ResolveAsync`** — after a successful repository read or open. The resolver's returned `Guid sessionKey` is the public handle; the consumer-facing `AnalyticsSession` projection is constructed alongside and written to the request-scoped `AnalyticsEventStateStore` via `SetCurrentSession` (when the request scope is reachable; same opportunistic pattern as slice-002's receipt).
2. **`AnalyzerSessionRepository.GetLatestActiveAsync`** — when projecting a DB row to an `AnalyticsSession?` for the resolver's cache-miss / race-collision retry path. The DTO → record mapping is a straight-line property copy.

No public constructor variant beyond the positional one. Consumers don't construct `AnalyticsSession` themselves — they consume what `CurrentSession` returns.

## Tests proving conformance

| Test | Asserts | Per spec acceptance scenario / SC |
|---|---|---|
| `PublicSurfacePinningTests.SnapshotMatchesBaseline` | Reflection canonical form matches the regenerated baseline (includes `AnalyticsSession` as a new public type block). | SC-007 |
| `Integration/Sessions/ResolveAndAttachTests.SessionProjectionExposedThroughStateProvider` | An in-process consumer reading `IAnalyticsEventStateProvider.CurrentSession` sees a populated `AnalyticsSession` with `SessionKey` == the persisted row's `sessionKey`, `StartUtc` == receipt time, `PageviewCount` == 1 (first pageview). | US1 AS1 |
| `Integration/Sessions/ResolveAndAttachTests.SessionProjectionAdvancesOnExtend` | After three pageviews, `CurrentSession.PageviewCount = 3`, `LastActivityUtc` matches the third pageview's `receivedUtc`. | US1 AS2 |
| Pinning baseline regen has Sync Impact note in `spec.md` Assumptions | The slice's PR includes the baseline diff + the spec acknowledgement; reviewers can see both. | Process gate (Principle X) |

## Versioning

`AnalyticsSession` is **public, pinned, and stable** at slice 003. Adding properties is allowed per Principle X — done via `init`-only properties appended to the record body (matches the `AnalyticsEventReceipt.SessionKey` pattern in slice 003 itself). Removing or renaming properties is PROHIBITED outside a MAJOR release.

When a future slice surfaces device dimensions on the session (proposed slice ~006 or later), the additive properties land as init-only members; the positional constructor signature stays as the 7-tuple above. If that slice needs to add a positional constructor parameter, that's a MAJOR release and the slice owner produces a constitution-amendment-shape justification.
